services.name: pub.client.sftp:login
description: WmPublic. Creates a secure connection to the SFTP server using the specified user alias or configuration parameters.

input_parameters.name: userAlias
description: Integration Server creates a new session each time it logs in to the SFTP server and returns the session key to the user.

Note: It is recommended to use userAlias instead of the fields within the sftpConfigurationParameters document because it is resilient to changes in configuration, such as removal of algorithms or ciphers in favor of stronger algorithms and ciphers.
If you provide inputs for both userAlias and SFTP configuration parameters, then userAlias takes precedence.

input_parameters.name: reuseSession
description: - true, to reuse the session that is already open for the specified user alias. If a session is not open for this user alias, the pub.client.sftp:login service creates a new session and returns the key for this new session.
- false, to create a new session for the specified user alias. This is the default.

input_parameters.name: sftpConfigurationParameters
description: Optional. The following parameters are part of this document.

input_parameters.name: sftpClientVersion
description: Indicates the Integration Server SFTP client version, which can be version1 (existing) or version2 (new). Set to version2, if you want Integration Server to use the newer SFTP client that has new configuration properties, additional Key Exchange Algorithms, Machine Access Code (MAC) algorithms, and ciphers.

input_parameters.name: serverHost
description: The IP address or the host name of the SFTP server.

input_parameters.name: serverPort
description: The SFTP server port number. The port number must be within the range of 0 and 65535 (inclusive).

children.name: hostKeyBytes
description: Note: You can get the host key of the SFTP server by using the pub.client.sftp.admin:getHostKey service.

input_parameters.name: userName
description: The user name for the SFTP user account.

input_parameters.name: authenticationType
description: - password, to use password-based authentication.

- public key, to use public key-based authentication. If the authentication type is password, then the value in the password field is used. If the authentication type is publicKey, then the value provided in the privateKeyBytes field is used.

children.name: password
description: Optional. Password of the specified SFTP user account. This is required if authenticationType is password.

input_parameters.name: privateKeyBytes
description: (an array of bytes (byte[])). Optional. If the authentication type is publicKey, then provide the private key of the specified SFTP user account.

children.name: passPhrase
description: Optional. It is required if you select publicKey as the authentication type and the private key that you specified requires a passphrase

input_parameters.name: minDHKeySize
description: Optional. The minimum DH key size. The default value is 1024. This field is not applicable if sftpClientVersion is version1.

input_parameters.name: maxDHKeySize
description: Optional. The maximum DH key size. The default value is 8192. This field is not applicable, if sftpClientVersion is version1.

children.name: preferredKeyExchangeAlgorithms
description: Note: You can get the list of default key exchange algorithms, MAC algorithms, and ciphers by using the pub.client.sftp.admin:getDefaultAlgorithms service.

input_parameters.name: preferredCiphersS2C
description: Optional. A comma-separated list of preferred server-to-client ciphers. If no value is provided, then the default list of server-to-client ciphers (in the default order) is used.

children.name: preferredCiphersC2S
description: Optional. A comma-separated list of preferred client-to-server ciphers. If a value is not provided, then the default list of client-to-server ciphers (in the default order) is used.

input_parameters.name: preferredMacC2S
description: Optional. A comma-separated list of preferred client-to-server Message Authentication Code (MAC) algorithms. If a value is not provided, then the default list of client-to-server MAC algorithms (in the default order) is used.

children.name: preferredMacS2C
description: Optional. A comma-separated list of preferred server-to-client Message Authentication Code (MAC) algorithms. If a value is not provided, then the default list of server-to-client MAC algorithms (in the default order) is used.

input_parameters.name: proxyAlias
description: Optional. The proxy alias through which the request should be routed. The proxy alias can be HTTP, HTTPS, or SOCKS. If a proxy alias is not specified, Integration Server makes outbound requests using each enabled proxy server alias until the request is sent successfully or all proxy servers are tried.

children.name: maximumRetries
description: Optional. The maximum number of times Integration Server tries to connect to the SFTP server, if login fails. The default value is 5. This field is applicable, if sftpClientVersion is version1.

input_parameters.name: connectionTimeout
description: Optional. The time (in seconds) that Integration Server waits for a response from the SFTP server before timing out and terminating the request. The default is 0, which indicates that the session never times out.

children.name: sessionTimeout
description: Optional. The number of minutes that Integration Server waits before terminating an idle session. The default is 30 minutes.

input_parameters.name: compression
description: Note: Use compression, only if the SFTP server to which you connect supports compression.

children.name: compressionLevel
description: Optional. The compression level to use, if you select zlib in the compression field. The minimum allowed value is 1 (fast, minimum compression). The maximum allowed value is 6 (slow, maximum compression). The default value is 6. This field is not applicable if sftpClientVersion is version2.

input_parameters.name: strictHostKeyChecking
description: - If set to Yes and the host key does not match the host key provided in the hostKeyBytes field at the time of handshake, then the connection fails.
- If set to No, then the host key is not checked during the handshake.

output_parameters.name: sessionKey
description: String. On successful login, this field contains the session key.

output_parameters.name: returnCode
description: String. The code returned for the login command.

output_parameters.name: returnMsg
description: The message returned for the login command.

usage_notes: You cannot execute SFTP commands in parallel using the same session key.

If the watt.client.sftp.session.logoutOnServiceCompletion parameter is set to true, the FTP session created by the pub.client.sftp:login service closes automatically when the invoking service completes execution. If the value is set to false, then the pub.client.sftp:logout service must be invoked to close the FTP(S) session.

services.name: pub.client.sftp.admin:getDefaultAlgorithms
description: Gets the default list of algorithms supported by the Integration Server SFTP client. It returns the default values for the following parameters: keyExchangeAlgorithms, ciphersC2S, ciphersS2C, macC2S, and macS2C. The values are returned as a comma-separated list.

input_parameters.name: sftpClientVersion
description: Indicates the Integration Server SFTP client version, which can be version1(existing) or version2(newer). Set to version2, if you want Integration Server to use the newer SFTP client that has new configuration properties, additional Key Exchange Algorithms, Machine Access Code (MAC) algorithms, and ciphers.

output_parameters.name: keyExchangeAlgorithms
description: A comma-separated list of supported Key Exchange Algorithms.

output_parameters.name: ciphersC2S
description: A comma-separated list of supported client-to-server ciphers.

output_parameters.name: ciphersS2C
description: A comma-separated list of supported server-to-client ciphers.

output_parameters.name: macC2S
description: A comma-separated list of supported client-to-server Message Authentication Code (MAC) algorithms.

output_parameters.name: macS2C
description: A comma-separated list of supported server-to-client Message Authentication Code (MAC) algorithms.

services.name: pub.client.sftp.admin:getHostKey
description: WmPublic. Gets the host key of the SFTP server.

input_parameters.name: sftpClientVersion
description: Indicates the Integration Server SFTP client version, which can be version1(existing) or version2(newer). Set to version2, if you want Integration Server to use the newer SFTP client that has new configuration properties, additional Key Exchange Algorithms, Machine Access Code (MAC) algorithms, and ciphers.

input_parameters.name: serverHost
description: The IP address or the host name of the SFTP server.

input_parameters.name: serverPort
description: The SFTP server port number. The port number must be within the range of 0 and 65535 (inclusive).

input_parameters.name: preferredKeyExchangeAlgorithms
description: Note: You can get the list of default key exchange algorithms, MAC algorithms, and ciphers by using the pub.client.sftp.admin:getDefaultAlgorithms service.

input_parameters.name: preferredCiphersS2C
description: Optional. A comma-separated list of preferred server-to-client ciphers. If no value is provided, then the default list of server-to-client ciphers (in the default order) is used.

input_parameters.name: preferredCiphersC2S
description: Optional. A comma-separated list of preferred client-to-server ciphers. If a value is not provided, then the default list of client-to-server ciphers (in the default order) is used.

input_parameters.name: preferredMacC2S
description: Optional. A comma-separated list of preferred client-to-server Message Authentication Code (MAC) algorithms. If a value is not provided, then the default list of client-to-server MAC algorithms (in the default order) is used.

input_parameters.name: preferredMacS2C
description: Optional. A comma-separated list of preferred server-to-client Message Authentication Code (MAC) algorithms. If a value is not provided, then the default list of server-to-client MAC algorithms (in the default order) is used.

input_parameters.name: minDHKeySize
description: Optional. The minimum DH key size. The default value is 1024. This field is not applicable if sftpClientVersion is version1.

input_parameters.name: maxDHKeySize
description: Optional. The maximum DH key size. The default value is 8192. This field is not applicable, if sftpClientVersion is version1.

input_parameters.name: proxyAlias
description: Optional. The proxy alias through which the request should be routed. The proxy alias can be HTTP, HTTPS, or SOCKS. If a proxy alias is not specified, Integration Server makes outbound requests using each enabled proxy server alias until the request is sent successfully or all proxy servers are tried.

output_parameters.name: hostKeyBytes
description: An array of bytes (byte[]). The host key in byte array format.

output_parameters.name: fingerprint
description: Fingerprint of the host key.

services.name: pub.client.sftp:logout
description: WmPublic. Logs off the user from the SFTP server and ends the current SFTP session.

input_parameters.name: sessionKey
description: Unique key for the current SFTP session. The sessionKey parameter is returned by the pub.client.sftp:login service.

output_parameters.name: returnCode
description: Standard SFTP protocol return code.

output_parameters.name: returnMsg
description: Text message describing the return code.

usage_notes: You cannot execute SFTP commands in parallel using the same session key.

services.name: pub.client.sftp:ls
description: WmPublic. Retrieves the remote directory listing of the specified path. If path is not specified, the pub.client.sftp:ls service retrieves the file listing of the current remote directory. The pub.client.sftp:ls service also retrieves additional details such as permissions and ownership information.

input_parameters.name: sessionKey
description: Note: sessionKey is required for connections in webMethods Integration Server (on-premises), but optional for connections created on Edge runtimes and cloud environments.

input_parameters.name: $connectionAlias
description: Note: This parameter is not applicable to SFTP connections in webMethods Integration Server (on-premises).

input_parameters.name: path
description: You can use the wildcard characters asterisk (*) and question mark (?) after the last slash mark (/) to view all remote directories that match the specified path.
Note: SFTP client version 2 supports only the asterisk (*) wildcard for filtering files. For example, *.xml , *.txt , and so on. However, Java regular expressions are not supported.

output_parameters.name: returnCode
description: Standard SFTP protocol return code.

output_parameters.name: returnMsg
description: Text message describing the return code.

output_parameters.name: dirList
description: List of directories matching the pattern specified in the path parameter. Following are the parameters in the document:

children.name: fileName
description: Specifies the name of the remote file.

children.name: fileSize
description: Specifies the size of the remote file.

children.name: permissions
description: Specifies the access permission of the file (read, write, or execute).

children.name: lastAccessTime
description: Specifies the time when the file was last accessed.

children.name: lastModifiedTime
description: Specifies the time when the file was last modified.

children.name: uid
description: Specifies the user ID of the file owner.

children.name: gid
description: Specifies the group id associated with the file.

children.name: longName
description: Specifies the long name of the ls entry. It contains all the parameters space separated.

usage_notes: You cannot execute SFTP commands in parallel using the same session key.
The date format for SFTP client services is determined by the watt.server.sftp.dateStampFmt property.

services.name: pub.client.sftp:mkdir
description: WmPublic. Creates a new remote directory.

input_parameters.name: sessionKey
description: Note: sessionKey is required for connections in webMethods Integration Server (on-premises), but optional for connections created on Edge runtimes and cloud environments.

input_parameters.name: $connectionAlias
description: Note: This parameter is not applicable to SFTP connections in webMethods Integration Server (on-premises).

input_parameters.name: path
description: Absolute or relative path of the remote directory where you want to create a new directory.

output_parameters.name: returnCode
description: Standard SFTP protocol return code.

output_parameters.name: returnMsg
description: Text message describing the return code.

usage_notes: You cannot execute SFTP commands in parallel using the same session key.

services.name: pub.client.sftp:put
description: WmPublic. Transfers a file to a remote SFTP server.

input_parameters.name: sessionKey
description: Note: sessionKey is required for connections in webMethods Integration Server (on-premises), but optional for connections created on Edge runtimes and cloud environments.

input_parameters.name: $connectionAlias
description: Note: This parameter is not applicable to SFTP connections in webMethods Integration Server (on-premises).

input_parameters.name: contentStream
description: Optional. Data to be transferred to the remote file.

input_parameters.name: localFile
description: Optional. Name of the local file to be appended to the remote file. Use localFile only if contentStream is not specified.

input_parameters.name: remoteFile
description: Optional. Absolute or relative path of the remote file to which the local file is to be appended.

input_parameters.name: mode
description: - overwrite to overwrite the contents of the remote file with the contents of the local file. This is the default.
- append to append the entire contents of the local file to the remote file.
- resume to resume writing the contents of the local file to the remote file from the point the writing was stopped during previous SFTP sessions.

input_parameters.name: remoteFileNameEncoding
description: Optional. Character set in which the remote file is encoded. The default is UTF-8.

input_parameters.name: localFileNameEncoding
description: Optional. Character set in which the local file is encoded. The default is UTF-8.

output_parameters.name: returnCode
description: Standard SFTP protocol return code.

output_parameters.name: returnMsg
description: Text message describing the return code.

usage_notes: If you specify contentStream, you must specify remoteFile. In this case, localFile is optional.
If you specify localFile, then remoteFile and contentStream are optional. In this case, the remote file will be given the same name as the local file.
You cannot execute SFTP commands in parallel using the same session key.

services.name: pub.client.sftp:pwd
description: WmPublic. Displays the remote working directory in the SFTP server.

input_parameters.name: sessionKey
description: Note: sessionKey is required for connections in webMethods Integration Server (on-premises), but optional for connections created on Edge runtimes and cloud environments.

input_parameters.name: $connectionAlias
description: Note: This parameter is not applicable to SFTP connections in webMethods Integration Server (on-premises).

output_parameters.name: returnCode
description: Standard SFTP protocol return code.

output_parameters.name: returnMsg
description: Text message describing the return code.

output_parameters.name: path
description: Absolute or relative path of the working directory on the remote SFTP server.

usage_notes: You cannot execute SFTP commands in parallel using the same session key.

services.name: pub.client.sftp:rename
description: WmPublic. Renames a file or directory on a remote SFTP server.

input_parameters.name: sessionKey
description: Note: sessionKey is required for connections in webMethods Integration Server (on-premises), but optional for connections created on Edge runtimes and cloud environments.

input_parameters.name: $connectionAlias
description: Note: This parameter is not applicable to SFTP connections in webMethods Integration Server (on-premises).

input_parameters.name: oldPath
description: Fully qualified name of the file you want to rename (for example, temp/oldname.txt).

input_parameters.name: newPath
description: New fully qualified name for the file (for example, temp/newname.txt).

output_parameters.name: returnCode
description: Standard SFTP protocol return code.

output_parameters.name: returnMsg
description: Text message describing the return code.

usage_notes: You cannot execute SFTP commands in parallel using the same session key.

services.name: pub.client.sftp:rm
description: WmPublic. Deletes one or more remote files on the SFTP server.

input_parameters.name: sessionKey
description: Note: sessionKey is required for connections in webMethods Integration Server (on-premises), but optional for connections created on Edge runtimes and cloud environments.

input_parameters.name: $connectionAlias
description: Note: This parameter is not applicable to SFTP connections in webMethods Integration Server (on-premises).

input_parameters.name: path
description: Absolute or relative path of the file you want to delete.

output_parameters.name: returnCode
description: Standard SFTP protocol return code.

output_parameters.name: returnMsg
description: Text message describing the return code.

usage_notes: You cannot execute SFTP commands in parallel using the same session key.

services.name: pub.client.sftp:rmdir
description: WmPublic. Deletes one or more remote directories on the SFTP server.

input_parameters.name: sessionKey
description: Note: sessionKey is required for connections in webMethods Integration Server (on-premises), but optional for connections created on Edge runtimes and cloud environments.

input_parameters.name: $connectionAlias
description: Note: This parameter is not applicable to SFTP connections in webMethods Integration Server (on-premises).

input_parameters.name: path
description: Absolute or relative path of the directory you want to delete.

output_parameters.name: returnCode
description: Standard SFTP protocol return code.

output_parameters.name: returnMsg
description: Text message describing the return code.

usage_notes: The remote directories that you want to delete must be empty.
You cannot execute SFTP commands in parallel using the same session key.

services.name: pub.client.sftp:symlink
description: WmPublic. Creates a symbolic link between the old path and the new path of a file.

input_parameters.name: sessionKey
description: Note: sessionKey is required for connections in webMethods Integration Server (on-premises), but optional for connections created on Edge runtimes and cloud environments.

input_parameters.name: $connectionAlias
description: Note: This parameter is not applicable to SFTP connections in webMethods Integration Server (on-premises).

input_parameters.name: oldPath
description: Old path of the file for which you want to create a symbolic link.

input_parameters.name: newPath
description: New path of the file to which the symbolic link should point.

output_parameters.name: returnCode
description: Standard SFTP protocol return code.

output_parameters.name: returnMsg
description: Text message describing the return code.

usage_notes: You cannot execute SFTP commands in parallel using the same session key.

services.name: pub.client:smtp
description: WmPublic. Sends a MIME-type e-mail message.
You may attach one or more content objects or files to the message.

input_parameters.name: to
description: Optional. E-mail address of the receiver. If you specify multiple addresses, separate them with commas.

input_parameters.name: cc
description: Optional. E-mail addresses of additional receivers. If you specify multiple addresses, separate them with commas.

input_parameters.name: bcc
description: Optional. E-mail addresses of additional receivers. If you specify multiple addresses, separate them with commas.

input_parameters.name: subject
description: Optional. Subject of the message.

input_parameters.name: subjectCharset
description: Optional. The character set used to encode the MIME message headers (including subject). If subjectCharset is not specified, then charset is used. If charset is not specified, the value in the server configuration parameter watt.server.email.charset is used. If that parameter is not set, the utf-8 encoding is used.

input_parameters.name: charset
description: Optional. The character encoding of the body text. If you do not specify the value of charset, the value in the server configuration parameter watt.server.email.charset is used. If that parameter is not set, the utf-8 encoding is used.

input_parameters.name: from
description: Optional. E-mail address of the sender. If you do not specify a from value, Integration Server uses the value specified for the mail.smtp.from JVM property. If no value is specified for that property, Integration Server uses the default value, user@servername, where user is the operating system user ID, and servername is the host name of the Integration Server.

input_parameters.name: mailhost
description: If no value is provided for the mailhost parameter, Integration Server uses the value of the Java system property mail.smtp.host set for Integration Server as the mailhost value. For more information about setting Java system properties for Integration Server, which includes modifying the custom_wrapper.conf file, see Changing Settings in the Configuration File custom_wrapper.conf .
If no value is supplied for the mailhost parameter and the mail.smtp.host Java system property is not set, the pub.client:smtp service ends with an exception.
Important: mailhost is required for connections in Integration Server (on-premises), but optional for connections created on Edge runtimes and cloud environments.

references.title: Changing Settings in the Configuration File custom_wrapper.conf

input_parameters.name: mailhostPort
description: Optional. The number of the port on which the SMTP host listens. This parameter does not need to be set if the host listens on port 25 (the standard SMTP port).

input_parameters.name: auth
description: Optional. Authorization information that the SMTP service will submit.

children.name: type
description: - Select Basic to authenticate the user on the specified e-mail server with only username and password. This is the default authentication type.
- Select Bearer to authenticate the user on the specified e-mail server with the access token issued by the OAuth server.

children.name: user
description: The username that this service submits when requesting a protected resource.

children.name: pass
description: Conditional. Password associated with user. This parameter is required only when auth is Basic.

children.name: token
description: Conditional. The access token issued by the OAuth server to Integration Server. This parameter is required only when auth is Bearer. To get the access token, see pub.client.oauth:​getExternalAccessToken

input_parameters.name: secure
description: Optional. Parameters specifying the security protocol and truststore information for certificate validation that Integration Server uses when communicating with the SMTP server port.

children.name: transportLayerSecurity
description: - none to use a non-secure mode when communicating with the port on the SMTP server. This is the default.

- explicit to use explicit security when communicating with the port on the SMTP server. With explicit security, Integration Server establishes an un-encrypted connection to the e-mail server and then switches to the secure mode.
- implicit to use implicit security when communicating with the port on the SMTP server. With implicit security, Integration Server always establishes an encrypted connection to the e-mail server.

children.name: truststoreAlias
description: Optional. Alias for the truststore that contains the list of certificates that Integration Server uses to validate the trust relationship.If you do not specify a truststore alias, the default truststore alias will be used.

input_parameters.name: body
description: Optional. The content of the message.

input_parameters.name: mimeStream
description: Optional. MIME or S/MIME message that you want to send in the e-mail. A mimeStream is created by the pub.mime:getEnvelopeStream, pub.smime:createEncryptedData, pub.smime:createSignedData, or pub.smime.keystore:createSignedData services. It contains both headers and content. If the mimeStream already contains the from, to, and subject headers, you do not need to pass them as individual inputs to this service.

input_parameters.name: $connectionAlias
description: Note: This parameter is not applicable to SMTP connections in webMethods Integration Server (on-premises).

input_parameters.name: attachments
description: Optional. Attachments to the message. Each attachment defines one message part in a multi-part message.

children.name: contenttype
description: application/x-edi-message

children.name: content
description: Content of the message.

children.name: filename
description: - If you specify attachment/content and attachments/filename, the service uses the value of attachments/filename as the name to assign to the attachment specified by attachment/content .
- If you specify attachments/filename, but not attachment/content, the service attaches the local file specified by attachments/filename.

children.name: encoding
description: Optional. Encoding of the message. For example: base64 or 7bit. If encoding is not specified, 7bit is used.

children.name: charset
description: Optional. Character set encoding of the attachment. This value is added to the Content-Type header for the attachment. If charset is not specified, the value in the server configuration parameter watt.server.email.charset is used. If that parameter is not set, the utf-8 encoding is used.

input_parameters.name: properties
description: Optional. SMTP system properties to send to the SMTP server. This parameter overrides the settings on the watt.config.systemProperties server configuration parameter. If you omit this properties parameter, Integration Server uses the settings specified on the watt.config.systemProperties server configuration parameter.

children.name: name
description: Name of the SMTP system property.

children.name: value
description: Value to specify for the SMTP property.

output_parameters.name: status
description: Final status of service.

usage_notes: Any one of the recipient fields, that is the to, cc, or the bcc parameter, must be defined.

If you are using filename to attach a file to the message and the file is not a plain text file, you must set the contenttype and encoding. For example, to attach IntegrationServer_directory\instances\instance_name\mydir\myfile.doc to a pub.client:smtp service, you would invoke the service with the following values in attachments:
contenttype:application/msword
filename:instances/instance_name/mydir/myfile.doc
encoding:base64

Prior to Integration Server version 10.15, the pub.client:smtp service included the useJSSE input parameter which specified whether Integration Server created the outbound connection using the Java Secure Socket Extension (JSSE) library. Beginning with Integration Serverversion 10.15, Integration Server establishes all secure outbound connections with JSSE. If you migrated to Integration Server 10.15 and a migrated service invokes pub.client:smtp, the useJSSE parameter still appears in the pipeline. However, Integration Server ignores the value of the useJSSE parameter , proceeding as if useJSSE were set to yes.

services.name: pub.client:soapClient
description: WmPublic. Creates and sends SOAP 1.1 and SOAP 1.2 messages over HTTP, HTTPS, or JMS transports for any style/use combination supported by Integration Server.

input_parameters.name: address
description: http://rubicon:5555/soap/ws/example:calculator

input_parameters.name: request
description: The input parameters that are to be passed to the web service.

input_parameters.name: wsdName
description: The name of the consumer web service descriptor that contains the operation you want to invoke.

input_parameters.name: wsdBinderName
description: Note: The consumer web service endpoint alias assigned to a binder indicates which proxy server Integration Server uses to send the request. For more information about proxy server usage and web service endpoint aliases, see Creating an Endpoint Alias for a Provider Web Service Descriptor for Use... .

references.title: Creating an Endpoint Alias for a Provider Web Service Descriptor for Use...

input_parameters.name: wsdOperationName
description: The name of the operation that you want to invoke. This operation must be contained in the binder specified in wsdBinderName.

input_parameters.name: targetInputSignature
description: Fully qualified name of the IS document type to use to validate and encode the contents of request.

input_parameters.name: targetOutputSignature
description: Fully qualified name of the IS document type to use to validate and decode the output value returned by the web service.

input_parameters.name: soapHeaders
description: Optional. Header documents included in the SOAP request as SOAP headers.

input_parameters.name: transportHeaders
description: Specify a key in transportHeaders for each header field that you want to set, where the key's name represents the name of the header field and the key's value represents the value of that header field.
The names and values supplied to transportHeaders must be of type String. If a transport header has a name or value that is not of type String, the header will not be included in the message.
The headers that you pass in to transportHeaders vary depending on the transport used to send the SOAP message. The supplied wsdBinderName determines the transport.
For more information about specifying transportHeaders, refer to the Usage Notes.

input_parameters.name: method
description: Optional. The QName of the requested procedure. The name is defined as follows:

children.name: namespaceName
description: Namespace portion of the procedure's QName.

children.name: localName
description: Local portion of the procedure's QName.

input_parameters.name: auth
description: Integration Server allows two levels of authorization credentials: transport level and message level.
Each element is defined as follows:

children.name: transport
description: Optional. Transport level authorization parameters.

children.name: type
description: - Basic to submit a user name and password. This is the default.
- Digest to submit a password digest for authentication.
- NTLM to use NTLM authentication.

children.name: user
description: Note: If you have specified NTLM as type, you must specify user in the following format: domain_name\user_name

children.name: pass
description: Optional. Password that this service will submit if one is requested.

children.name: serverCerts
description: privateKey Object The SOAP message signer's private key.
certChain Object List A list containing the signer's complete certificate chain, where element 0 in the list contains the signer's certificate and element 1 contains the CA's certificate.

children.name: message
description: Optional. Message level authorization parameters.

children.name: user
description: Optional. User name that this service will use if one is requested.

children.name: pass
description: Optional. Password that this service will submit if one is requested.

children.name: serverCerts
description: privateKey Object The SOAP message signer's private key.
certChain Object List A list containing the signer's complete certificate chain, where element 0 in the list contains the signer's certificate and element 1 contains the CA's certificate.

children.name: partnerCert
description: Optional. The partner's complete certificate chain, where element 0 in the list contains the message signer's certificate and element 1 contains the CA's certificate.

input_parameters.name: timeout
description: A value of 0 means Integration Server waits for a response indefinitely. If the connection to the host or JMS provider ends before Integration Server receives a response, the service ends with an exception and a status code of 408.
If this parameter is not specified, or an invalid (non-numeric) value is specified, Integration Server uses one of the following values:
- For HTTP, Integration Server uses the value of the watt.server.SOAP.request.timeout server configuration parameter as the timeout value
- For JMS, Integration Server uses the value of the watt.server.soapjms.request.timeout server configuration parameter as the timeout value.
For more information about watt.server.SOAP.request.timeout and watt.server.soapjms.request.timeout server configuration parameter, see IBM webMethods Integration Server Administrator’s Guide .
Integration Server ignores timeout if the name/value pair jms.async= true is passed in to transportHeaders.

input_parameters.name: soapAction
description: - If soapProtocol is set to SOAP 1.1 Protocol, specifies the value to which you want to set the SOAPAction HTTP header.
- If soapProtocol is set to SOAP 1.2 Protocol, specifies the value to which you want to set the action attribute in the Content-Type header.
Integration Server ignores soapAction in either of the following situations:
- The Content-Type header is passed in to transportHeaders and soapProtocol is set to SOAP 1.2 Protocol.
- The soapAction header is passed into transportHeaders and soapProtocol is set to SOAP 1.1 Protocol.

input_parameters.name: soapProtocol
description: Optional. Indicates the SOAP protocol the service uses to send messages. Valid values are SOAP 1.1 Protocol or SOAP 1.2 Protocol.

input_parameters.name: encoding
description: Integration Server ignores encoding if the Content-Type header is passed in to transportHeaders.

output_parameters.name: soapResponseData
description: A SOAP object containing the SOAP response message returned by the server specified in address.

output_parameters.name: response
description: Output parameters returned from the web service.

output_parameters.name: header
description: header contains the following keys:

children.name: requestLines
description: The contents of the requestLines document are not identical to transportHeaders. The transport can add, remove, or alter specific headers while processing the request.

Whether or not the web service connector returns the requestLines parameter depends on the success or failure of the pub.client:soapClient service. In the case of failure, the point at which the failure occurs determines the presence of the requestLines parameter. For more information, see the usage notes for this service.

children.name: lines
description: Whether or not the pub.client:soapClient service returns the lines parameter depends on the success or failure of the service. In the case of failure, the point at which the failure occurs determines the presence of the lines parameter. For more information, see the usage notes for this service.
For the HTTP or HTTPS transports, the lines parameter contains any HTTP/HTTPS headers present in the response.
For the JMS transport, the lines parameter contains the JMS headers present in the response.

children.name: status
description: Status code from the request, returned by the underlying transport. response. For more information about status codes returned by the service, see the usage notes for this service.

children.name: statusMessage
description: Status message returned by the transport. For more information about status messages returned by the service, see the usage notes for this service.

output_parameters.name: soapStatus
description: Flag indicating whether the SOAP request message was processed successfully.
A value of 0 indicates that The remote server successfully processed the SOAP request and returned a SOAP response message.
A value of 1 indicates that The remote server returned a SOAP fault, indicating that the SOAP request was received but was not processed successfully.

A value of 2 indicates that The server returned an error that was not a SOAP fault. This indicates that some type of HTTP error occurred (often, an HTTP 404). You can check the status field in header to determine the type of HTTP error that occurred.

usage_notes: If the address begins with https:, you must specify a private key and certificate chain. You can use the auth/transport/serverCerts parameters to do so. If you do not specify them using the auth/transport/serverCerts parameters, pub.client:soapClient uses the web service endpoint alias specified in the binder. If the endpoint alias does not have an associated private key and certificate chain, then the default outbound SSL certificate settings are used to authenticate the resources.
Note: pub.client:soapClient supports NTLM protected web service over HTTP as well as HTTPS.
As part of executing pub.client:soapClient, Integration Server executes any service handlers assigned to the consumer web service descriptor specified in wsdName.

When a document type contains a String variable that represents a required attribute (meaning that the variable name starts with the "@" symbol and the Required property is set to True in Designer) and the input document does not contain the required attribute, Integration Server adds an empty attribute during document encoding. For example, if the document type contains a required String variable named @myAttribute but @myAttribute is missing from the input document, Integration Server adds myAttribute ="" to the XML document.

Note: Because empty xmlns attributes are invalid, if the document type contains a required String variable named @xmlns and the input document does not specify a value for the @xmlns attribute, Integration Server does not add xmlns="" to the XML document.

services.name: pub.client:soapHTTP
description: WmPublic. Deprecated - There is no replacement service.
Submits a SOAP message to a server via HTTP or HTTPS.

input_parameters.name: soapRequestData
description: SOAP message that is to be sent. This object must be produced with the services in the soap folder. See the Usage Notes for more information.

input_parameters.name: address
description: https://servername:5555/soap/default

input_parameters.name: auth
description: Optional. Parameters specifying the credentials that are to be submitted to the server specified in address. Each element is defined as follows:

children.name: type
description: Type of authentication that the service will perform. Leave this field blank, as the only option currently available is basic HTTP authentication.

children.name: user
description: User name that this service will use if one is requested.

children.name: pass
description: Password that this service will submit if one is requested.

input_parameters.name: validateSOAP
description: - true to validate the response message and throw an exception if the response does not conform to the SOAP schema.
- false to bypass the validation process. This is the default.

input_parameters.name: SOAPAction
description: Optional. Value to which you want to set the SOAPAction HTTP header.

input_parameters.name: contentType
description: - text/xml; charset="utf-8" to specify the content type as XML and the character encoding of the message text as UTF-8. This is the default.
- text/xml to specify the content type as XML. Since the charset parameter is not specified, the character encoding of the message text defaults to US-ASCII.

input_parameters.name: loadAs
description: - stream to return the body of the response as a java.io.InputStream. Use this option when you will invoke an HTTP web service. This is the default for an HTTP service.

- bytes to return the body of the response as a byte[ ]. Use this option if the body will be used as input to a service that operates on whole HTML or XML documents (for example, pub.xml:queryXMLNode).

- byteArrayStream to have the response stream fully read and converted to java.io.ByteArrayStream. This prevents data loss or a truncated SOAP response if the connection closes prematurely. Use this option when you will invoke an HTTPS web service. This is the default for an HTTPS service.

input_parameters.name: timeout
description: Optional. Time (measured in milliseconds) to wait for a response from the remote server before timing out and terminating the request. The default value is to wait forever.

input_parameters.name: encoding
description: The encoding parameter is used to override the encoding determined by the watt.server.netEncoding server configuration parameter. For more information about watt.server.netEncoding, see IBM webMethods Integration Server Administrator’s Guide .

output_parameters.name: soapResponseData
description: The SOAP response message returned by the server specified in address.

output_parameters.name: header
description: Conditional. Headers from the HTTP response. Will contain the following keys:

children.name: lines
description: Header fields from the HTTP response. Each key in lines represents a field (line) of the response header. Key names represent the names of header fields. The keys' values are Strings containing the values of the fields.

children.name: status
description: Status code from the HTTP response.

children.name: statusMessage
description: Status message from the HTTP response.

output_parameters.name: soapStatus
description: Flag indicating whether the SOAP request message was processed successfully.
A value of 0 indicates that The remote server successfully processed the SOAP request and returned a SOAP response message.
A value of 1 indicates that The remote server returned a SOAP fault, indicating that the SOAP request was received but was not processed successfully.

A value of 2 indicates that The server returned an error that was not a SOAP fault. This indicates that some type of HTTP error occurred (often, an HTTP 404). You can check the status element in header to determine the type of HTTP error that occurred.

usage_notes: This pub.client:soapHTTP service is deprecated. There is not a replacement service.

If address begins with https:, you can use pub.security.keystore:setKeyAndChain to specify the certificate chain. If you do not specify a certificate chain, pub.client:soapHTTP uses the default outbound SSL certificate settings to authenticate the resources.

To send a SOAP message with this service, you must first generate an empty SOAP object with the pub.soap.utils:createSoapData service and then populate it using services such as pub.soap.utils:addHeaderEntry and pub.soap.utils:addBodyEntry.

If the Docker image for a Microservices Runtime excludes the web services functionality, then the Docker image for the Microservices Runtime running in the Docker container cannot execute the pub.client:soapHTTPservice. Attempts to execute pub.client:soapHTTP result in a ServiceException.

services.name: pub.client:websocket
description: WmPublic. Establishes a WebSocket connection to the URL captured in the identified WebSocket client endpoint.

children.name: value
description: The value of the header

description: Authentication information that the pub.client:websocket service will submit to the URL in WebSocket client endpoint.

children.name: type
description: basic to submit a username and password.

children.name: username
description: Username that this service will submit when requesting a protected resource.

children.name: password
description: Password associated with user.

description: Optional. Parameters specifying the truststore information for certificate validation that Integration Serveruses when communicating with the WebSocket server port:
